outOfScope: Whole-program policy adequacy outside the declared corridor
scope.note: Synthetic public-safe ExampleCo proof bundle for a wider multi-framework corridor.

claims.title: Administrative identities require MFA and conditional access in the scoped production environment.
claims.basis: The synthetic identity export shows MFA required and conditional access enabled for scoped administrative identities.

claims.title: Infrastructure access in the scoped production environment requires unique named identities with no shared administrative accounts.
claims.basis: The identity export shows named accounts required for the scoped infrastructure access plane and no shared administrative accounts present.

claims.title: Scoped customer and environment boundaries are enforced with no undeclared cross-environment paths in the production slice.
claims.basis: The architecture export shows customer boundaries enforced, production separated from non-production, and no undeclared cross-environment paths in scope.

claims.title: Scoped password-quality controls enforce strong minimum length, no maximum-length restriction, and common-password blocking.
claims.basis: The identity export shows a minimum password length of at least 12 characters, no maximum-length restriction, and common-password blocking enabled for the scoped accounts.

claims.title: A managed web application firewall is attached to the scoped public ingress path, runs in blocking mode, and has a managed rule set active.
claims.basis: The edge export shows a managed web application firewall attached to the scoped public ingress path, running in blocking mode, with the managed rule set active.

claims.title: Audit logging is enabled with a declared retention window for the scoped production runtime.
claims.basis: The cloud logging export shows logging enabled and retention declared for the scoped runtime.

claims.title: Scoped runtime telemetry is forwarded to the declared central monitoring sink with detection rules enabled.
claims.basis: The monitoring export shows the scoped assets forwarding telemetry into the declared central sink with detection rules enabled.

claims.title: Public ingress is HTTPS-only with managed certificates and TLS 1.2 or higher.
claims.basis: The ingress export states HTTPS-only listeners, managed certificates, and a minimum TLS version of 1.2.

claims.title: The managed network boundary is attached to the declared ingress path for scoped public services.
claims.basis: The network boundary export shows the declared ingress path traverses the managed default-deny boundary.

claims.title: Administrative ingress is restricted to approved source ranges at the managed network boundary.
claims.basis: The network boundary export shows admin ingress restricted to declared source ranges with no unapproved admin exposure.

claims.title: Plaintext transport is disabled on the scoped public ingress path.
claims.basis: The ingress export shows HTTPS-only listeners and an explicit plaintext-disabled transport policy.

claims.title: Encryption at rest is enabled for the scoped customer data stores and no unencrypted store is present.
claims.basis: The storage export shows encryption enabled across the scoped customer data stores and no unencrypted store present in the declared slice.

claims.title: Scoped runtime service accounts do not use user-managed long-lived keys.
claims.basis: The IAM export states no user-managed keys are present for the scoped runtime service accounts.

claims.title: The scoped workload runs only in the declared approved regions.
claims.basis: The asset inventory export shows observed regions match the declared approved region list and no undeclared regions are present.

claims.title: Backup snapshots are scheduled with a declared immutable retention window.
claims.basis: The backup configuration export shows snapshots enabled with a 24-hour schedule and a declared immutable window.

claims.title: Security awareness completion is documented for the current review period.
claims.basis: The training claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named security manager.

claims.title: A restore exercise is documented inside the declared review window.
claims.basis: The restore exercise claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the platform lead.

claims.title: The processor register is current and DPA coverage is declared for the reviewed subprocessors.
claims.basis: The vendor register claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the privacy manager.

claims.title: The DSR runbook is approved and has a named owner for the current review window.
claims.basis: The DSR runbook claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the privacy manager.

claims.title: Privacy-by-design adequacy has been demonstrated for the full product change process.
claims.basis: This remains a human adequacy and interpretation zone that the public synthetic corridor does not reduce to a proof.

claims.title: A typed export of periodic access review evidence is present for the current review window.
claims.basis: The corridor expects a typed access-review export, but no such evidence is attached to this public synthetic pack.

claims.title: Default branch protections are enforced for the scoped production repository.
claims.basis: The repository export shows the default branch is protected, approvals are required, status checks are enforced, and force pushes are blocked.

claims.title: CI workflow policy constrains deployment to reviewed workflows and protected refs.
claims.basis: The CI export shows workflow review, trusted publishing, protected refs, and environment approvals are all required.

claims.title: The production change-review policy is approved and declares an emergency change path for the current review window.
claims.basis: The change-review governance claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named change owner.

claims.title: Access review closure and high-risk exception resolution are attested for the current review window.
claims.basis: The access-review closure claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named identity governance owner.

claims.title: Monitoring review cadence, ownership, and retention operations are attested for the scoped production telemetry program.
claims.basis: The monitoring-operations claim is documentary and signed by the named detection engineering owner.

claims.title: Configuration exceptions are current, owned, and not already expired in the current review window.
claims.basis: The configuration-exception claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named platform security owner.

claims.title: Patch exceptions declare compensating controls and no declared exception is already expired in the current review window.
claims.basis: The patch-exception claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named operations owner.

claims.title: Patch cadence and exception thresholds are adequate for the in-scope production assets.
claims.basis: This remains a human adequacy zone. The public corridor does not reduce patch timelines, exception thresholds, or compensating-control sufficiency to a theorem.

claims.title: The incident response runbook is approved, owned, and current for the declared review window.
claims.basis: The incident-runbook claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named security operations owner.

claims.title: Incident escalation contacts and notification paths are current for the declared review window.
claims.basis: The incident-contact claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named security operations owner.

claims.title: High-risk vendor agreements carry the required security and privacy terms for the current review window.
claims.basis: The vendor-terms claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named privacy and supplier-assurance owner.

claims.title: Vendor tiering, review cadence, and inherited-control allocation are adequate for the in-scope dependency set.
claims.basis: This remains a human adequacy zone. The public corridor does not pretend to prove that vendor tiering, review cadence, or inherited-control allocation are sufficient.

nextActions: Promote the remaining imported availability, vulnerability-management, and secure-engineering seed controls after the current identity, perimeter, and monitoring wave.
Keep privacy-by-design adequacy explicit as judgment rather than flattening it into a green check.
Pin the same inputs and outputs in the witness receipt for replay.
